title: Autocomplete — slash commands, skills, and file paths
purpose: Slash commands and file path references are the primary interaction surfaces beyond free-text prompts. This brief adds trigger-based autocomplete that renders as a popup within the fixed panel, letting users discover commands and reference files without memorising paths or command names.
task: Implement autocomplete in input/autocomplete.rs. Detect trigger characters: / at column 0 opens slash command completion (including skills), @ opens file/directory path completion. Build candidate lists from the SlashCommandRegistry (for / trigger, including source tags) and nucleo fuzzy matching against the working tree (for @ trigger). Render the popup inside the fixed panel, growing upward with DECSTBM reissue. Limit to 8 visible rows with overflow count. Up/Down navigate, Tab accepts, Escape dismisses, typing narrows candidates.

requirements.title: Trigger detection
requirements.spec: WHEN the user types / at column 0, THE SYSTEM SHALL open slash command autocomplete. WHEN the user types @, THE SYSTEM SHALL open file path autocomplete.
acceptance: / at column 0 (first character of input or first character after a newline) triggers slash autocomplete
/ in the middle of a word does not trigger autocomplete
@ anywhere in the input triggers file path autocomplete
detect_trigger(input, cursor_col) returns Option<AutocompleteTrigger> with the trigger type and prefix
Test: '/' at position 0 returns SlashCommand trigger
Test: 'text /' at position 5 does not trigger
Test: '@src' returns FilePath trigger with prefix 'src'

requirements.title: Slash command candidate generation
requirements.spec: WHEN slash command autocomplete is triggered, THE SYSTEM SHALL generate candidates from the SlashCommandRegistry with name, source tag, and description.
acceptance: Candidates include all registered slash commands (builtin + profile). Note: skill names depend on SkillSearchPaths being installed on the ToolContext (wired in builder.rs). If SkillSearchPaths is not installed, skill completion is unavailable — handle gracefully by omitting skills from candidates
Each candidate has: name, source_tag ('builtin' or 'profile'), description
Candidates are filtered by the prefix typed after /
Candidates are sorted alphabetically by name
Test: with prefix 'he', candidate list contains 'help' and not 'tools'

requirements.title: File path candidate generation
requirements.spec: WHEN file path autocomplete is triggered, THE SYSTEM SHALL generate candidates by fuzzy matching the prefix against the working tree using nucleo-matcher.
acceptance: File paths are collected from the working tree (respecting .gitignore via the ignore crate or equivalent)
Nucleo fuzzy matcher scores paths against the typed prefix
Results sorted by match score descending
Directories are visually distinguished from files (trailing / or distinct styling)
Test: prefix 'main' matches 'src/main.rs' with a positive score

requirements.title: Popup rendering in the fixed panel
requirements.spec: The autocomplete popup SHALL render inside the fixed panel, growing upward from the input area, with DECSTBM reissued to accommodate the popup height.
acceptance: AutocompletePopup struct holds: candidates, selected_index, visible_offset
render(row, width, writer, caps) writes the popup rows above the input area
The currently selected candidate is highlighted (reverse video or distinct background)
Opening the popup triggers DECSTBM reissue (scroll region shrinks to make room)
Closing the popup triggers DECSTBM reissue (scroll region grows back)
Test: popup with 5 candidates renders 5 rows plus the selection highlight

requirements.title: Popup size limit and overflow
requirements.spec: The autocomplete popup SHALL show at most 8 rows AND SHALL display a count indicator when additional candidates exist.
acceptance: When candidates exceed 8, only 8 are visible at a time
A count indicator (e.g. '4 more...') appears at the bottom of the popup when there are hidden candidates
Scrolling the selection past the visible window shifts the visible range
Test: 12 candidates show 8 visible plus '4 more...' indicator

requirements.title: Popup navigation and acceptance
requirements.spec: WHEN the autocomplete popup is open, Up/Down SHALL navigate candidates, Tab SHALL accept the selected candidate, and Escape SHALL dismiss the popup.
acceptance: Up arrow moves selection up (wraps to bottom at top)
Down arrow moves selection down (wraps to top at bottom)
Tab inserts the selected candidate's text at the trigger position, replacing the typed prefix
Escape closes the popup without inserting anything
Typing additional characters narrows the candidate list in real-time
If narrowing leaves zero candidates, the popup closes automatically
Test: Tab on selected candidate inserts its text and closes popup

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the input editor itself — that is NT-004
SHALL NOT implement agent name, profile name, or session name completion — @ is for files only per Tom's direction
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: Trigger detection identifies / at column 0 and @ correctly — verified by tests
Slash command candidates filtered by prefix — verified by test
File path fuzzy matching produces scored results — verified by test
Popup renders correct number of rows with overflow indicator — verified by test
Tab acceptance inserts correct text — verified by test
